raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The artifact chains a design claim (joint recognition-and-clarification objective) to an empirical conclusion ('this gain validates…'); comma-splice run-ons in paragraphs 2 and 4 and an inverted results-before-methods ordering create logical-flow issues that any substantive look would surface.","R1.3":"Specific quantitative claims (2.4 pp absolute WER reduction, 96.1% intent preservation) are asserted against named benchmarks, and a causal inference is drawn from them; checking whether the stated numbers actually support the causal claim is central to a technical review.","R1.4":"The final paragraph makes an explicit 'first…to the best of our knowledge' novelty claim for a streaming end-to-end joint objective; that claim directly invites delta-vs.-prior-work assessment.","R1.6":"'Take a look at this paragraph' most literally implicates prose review; the text contains multiple comma-splice constructions and an awkward passive chain in paragraph 4 that would be caught under a writing-polish reading."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you primarily seeking feedback on the prose quality and sentence-level writing (grammar, style, run-on sentences), rather than a substantive technical review of the claims, logical consistency, and novelty assertion?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact chains a design claim (joint recognition-and-clarification objective) to an empirical conclusion ('this gain validates…'); comma-splice run-ons in paragraphs 2 and 4 and an inverted results-before-methods ordering create logical-flow issues that any substantive look would surface.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims (2.4 pp absolute WER reduction, 96.1% intent preservation) are asserted against named benchmarks, and a causal inference is drawn from them; checking whether the stated numbers actually support the causal claim is central to a technical review.
rationale.R1.4: The final paragraph makes an explicit 'first…to the best of our knowledge' novelty claim for a streaming end-to-end joint objective; that claim directly invites delta-vs.-prior-work assessment.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' most literally implicates prose review; the text contains multiple comma-splice constructions and an awkward passive chain in paragraph 4 that would be caught under a writing-polish reading.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily seeking feedback on the prose quality and sentence-level writing (grammar, style, run-on sentences), rather than a substantive technical review of the claims, logical consistency, and novelty assertion?
